author: y4rr
content:
Plan: Swin-T's blocks currently use ferrotorch_nn::MultiheadAttention (standard global attention) — see ferrotorch-vision/src/models/swin.rs:6-9 'uses standard (global) multi-head attention from ferrotorch_nn instead of shifted-window attention'. Torchvision swin_t uses shifted-window attention with relative-position-bias tables. The named_parameters() schema therefore cannot match torchvision swin_t state_dict keys (no relative_position_bias_table / relative_position_index entries on the ferrotorch side; presence of standard MHA's in_proj_weight/in_proj_bias on the ferrotorch side that don't exist on torchvision).

Phase 3 (#996) migrated Swin's permute-pattern CPU-pull bugs (4 sites: stage downsample in/out, main forward post-patch-embed, IntermediateFeatures sibling) onto Tensor::permute(...).contiguous(). The CPU-pull bug is fixed. The architectural divergence (shifted-window attention) remains and is escalated here.

Acceptance: ferrotorch-nn or ferrotorch-vision gains a ShiftedWindowAttention module with relative-position-bias support; swin.rs swaps MultiheadAttention for it; swin_t_value_parity drops the #[ignore] and passes torch.allclose against tvm.swin_t(weights=None).

Out of scope here: any change to the Phase 3 permute migration.
